# Issue #685 — Scope-Bypass Inventory (route layer)

**Generated by:** `scripts/src/check-scope-bypass.mjs` (PR-1, report-only).
**Status:** machine-generated inventory. Do not hand-edit — re-run the scanner.

> This report supersedes the inventory in `docs/audit/SCOPE_NORMALIZATION_RCA_685.md`,
> which reported **17 files / 68 hits**. That figure came from a partial
> exploration sweep; a full scan of the route layer finds the numbers below.

## Summary

```
  scanned route files            85
  files with >=1 hand-rolled hit 82
  total "companyId" = $N hits    2313
  // scope-ok suppressed         0
```

## By SQL statement shape

| Shape | Hits | % | Migration meaning |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `list-scan` | 1194 | 51.6 | multi-row SELECT — **the buildScopedWhere normalization target** |
| `detail-fetch` | 799 | 34.5 | SELECT with an `id = $` predicate — legitimate single-row tenant guard |
| `write-guard` | 320 | 13.8 | UPDATE / DELETE WHERE clause — legitimate tenant guard |
| `insert` | 0 | 0.0 | companyId inside an INSERT — legitimate |
| `unknown` | 0 | 0.0 | no SQL verb within 120 lines — needs manual read |

Of all hits, **88** are dynamic query-builder fragments
(`let where = ...`, `conditions.push(...)`) — counted as `list-scan` because
they assemble a SELECT list query whose WHERE is built at runtime.

## Migration-relevant subset

Only `list-scan` hits are candidates for a `buildScopedWhere` migration. The
rest (`detail-fetch`, `write-guard`, `insert`) are correct tenant guards and
must be left alone.

| Category | Hits | ≈ RCA #685 class |
|---|---:|---|
| `list-scan`, plain `"companyId" = $` | 648 | A (safe mechanical swap) |
| `list-scan`, aliased `x."companyId" = $` | 489 | B (needs `companyColumn` override) |
| `list-scan` in a manual-review file | 57 | C (do NOT migrate) |
| **migration target total (A + B)** | **1137** | |

## Manual-review files (RCA #685 Category C)

Hand-rolled scope here is legitimate by design — portal own-token scope, session
bootstrap, intentionally cross-tenant admin, or regulatory PDPL export scoping.
These must never be migrated to `buildScopedWhere`.

## Limitations (regex heuristic)

- **Statement shape is heuristic.** Each hit is classified by walking up to the
  nearest SQL verb within 120 lines. A `"companyId" = $` inside a
  sub-SELECT of an UPDATE is classified `list-scan`/`detail-fetch`, not `write-guard`.
- **`list-scan` vs `detail-fetch`** turns on finding an `id = $` predicate in the
  statement window; a list query joined to another table on its id can be
  misread as `detail-fetch`.
- **Only `"companyId"` is scanned.** `"branchId"` predicates and unquoted
  `companyId` are out of scope for this pass.
- The scanner is **report-only** — it never fails CI. Treat the counts as a
  triage starting point, not a verified migration list.

## How a file leaves this report

A file's `list-scan` count drops as its list/report handlers are migrated to
`buildScopedWhere` (separate per-cluster PRs). When a file's `list-scan` count
reaches 0 it is no longer a migration target — its remaining `detail-fetch` /
`write-guard` hits are expected and correct.

## Refresh

```bash
node scripts/src/check-scope-bypass.mjs
```
